description: If everything relates to itself, then something relates to itself.
goal: (-> (forall x (App (App R x) x)) (exists y (App (App R y) y)))

guides.text: A **reflexive** relation is one where every element relates to itself: $R(x,x)$ for all $x$. Examples: 'is the same height as', 'is at least as old as'. The goal is trivial but illuminating: if everything is self-related, then there exists something self-related. From $\forall$ to $\exists$.
Assume reflexivity: everything is $R$-related to itself. Notice the binary [predicate|predicate] syntax: `(App (App R x) x)` — the same term $x$ appears in both argument positions.
Instantiate with a concrete term $a$ using `forall-elim`. If everything is $R$-related to itself, then $a$ is $R$-related to $a$. Pick any [term|term] you like.

From the concrete example $R(a,a)$, use `exists-intro` to [existential-generalisation|generalise]: replace the [term|term] $a$ with the [bound-variable|bound variable] $y$ to get $\exists y.\; R(y,y)$. The witness is $a$, the variable is $y$.
[discharge|Discharge] the reflexivity assumption to finish the [implication|implication].
